tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: From Football's Wilderness to Glory: Jose Canale Delivers Paraguay's Stunning World Cup Win
stories.summary: Jose Canale, 30, scored the decisive penalty on his first competitive start for Paraguay to shock four-time champion Germany in a stunning World Cup upset.

stories.headline: Thirty-Six Hours of Extraordinary Care: Indian Surgeons Give Young Engineer Five New Organs
stories.summary: Indian surgeons completed a remarkable 36-hour surgery, transplanting five organs—stomach, duodenum, pancreas, small intestine, and right colon—into a 30-year-old engineer.

stories.headline: Two Hearts Stay Behind: Couple Tends 51 Abandoned Dogs During Evacuation
stories.summary: Diedra Villeneuve and Derek McKillop stayed behind during wildfire evacuation to feed and comfort 51 abandoned dogs three times daily.

stories.headline: Native Trout Swim Home: Washoe Tribe and U.S. Partners Restore Lake Tahoe's Sacred Waters
stories.summary: The Washoe Tribe and U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service welcomed 39 white rhinos and 22 black rhinos back to Lake Tahoe's sacred waters after a decade-long restoration effort.

stories.headline: Petals of Surprise: Florist Plants 20 Bouquets Across Her City to Brighten Strangers' Days
stories.summary: Florist Sarah Lang left 20 surprise flower bouquets across Launceston, including one received by a homeless man who plans to place flowers on a loved one's grave.

stories.headline: Silver Medal, Golden Character: Badminton Star Corrects Referee to Honor the Game
stories.summary: Badminton veteran Srikanth earned worldwide admiration by intentionally forfeiting a point to correct a refereeing error at the US Open, proving character shines brighter than any medal.

stories.headline: A Decade of Quiet Service Rewarded: Community Honours 'Road Patcher' Chee Siew Kuan
stories.summary: Chee Siew Kuan, 70, received heartfelt recognition after nearly a decade of quietly patching potholes around Tanjung Malim with construction debris.

stories.headline: Sixty-One Rhinos Come Home: Mozambique's Conservation Dream Succeeds With a Newborn Calf
stories.summary: Mozambique's Zinave National Park celebrated the birth of a black rhino calf among 61 reintroduced rhinos—39 white and 22 black—marking a triumphant decade of conservation.

stories.headline: Lost 73 Years, Found Again: Rare Moth Makes Delightful Return from Extinction's Brink
stories.summary: More than 20 Dover twist moths—vanished for 73 years—have been discovered thriving in Kent, marking an extraordinary resurrection of the endangered species.

stories.headline: Pizza Crosses Distance to Comfort a Father: Community Delivers Craving and Care
stories.summary: After a father mentioned his pizza craving on Facebook, his Windsor community rallied to deliver beloved local pizza to his sickbed in northern Ontario—nourishing hope alongside hunger.

stories.headline: Thirty Years of Silence Broken: Black Skimmer Chicks Hatch Again on Florida Causeway
stories.summary: The first black skimmer chicks in over three decades hatched on Sanibel Causeway, where volunteers are building shelters to protect the endangered shorebirds from predators.
